Versa FlexVNF CLI Command Reference

Comprehensive command-line interface reference for Versa Operating System (VOS) on FlexVNF appliances. Auto-generated from live device ? help output — every parameter, keyword, and sub-command is documented.

VOS 22.1.4+ ConfD Framework 2076 Commands 26514 Parameters SSH → bash → cli

Overview

Software VersionVOS 22.1.4 and later
PlatformVersa FlexVNF (physical and virtual appliances)
CLI FrameworkConfD (Tail-f) management framework
Access MethodSSH → bash shell → cli command
Data SourceLive CLI ? help output extracted from VOS 22.1.4 device
This reference was auto-generated by parsing every ? help output from a live Versa FlexVNF running VOS 22.1.4. Every parameter, keyword, and sub-command found on the device is documented here.

CLI Modes

2 MODES
Operational Mode
Default mode entered after login. Monitoring, diagnostics, and system operations without modifying configuration.
Prompt
admin@hostname-cli>
Available Commands
CommandDescription
showDisplay system information, configuration, and operational state
requestSystem-level requests (reboot, restart, clear, diagnostics)
clearClear command history
pingPing an IPv4/IPv6 host
tracerouteTrace route to a host
mtrCombined ping + traceroute
tcpdumpPacket capture on interfaces
monitorReal-time debugging and log monitoring
fileFile operations (copy, delete, list, show, compare, rename)
setSet CLI session properties (paginate, screen, history)
compareCompare running configuration
commitConfirm a pending commit
configureEnter configuration mode
ssh / scp / wgetRemote connectivity and file transfer
shellDrop to Linux bash shell
exit / quitExit management session
Configuration Mode
Allows modification of device configuration. Enter by typing configure from operational mode.
Prompt
admin@hostname-cli(config)%
Available Commands
CommandDescription
setSet a configuration parameter
deleteDelete a configuration element
editNavigate into a sub-element for editing
showShow configuration at current level
commitCommit current set of changes
compareShow configuration differences
validateValidate configuration without committing
rollbackRoll back to last committed version
load / saveLoad/save configuration from/to ASCII file
describeDisplay YANG model details for a config path
runRun an operational-mode command from config mode
top / up / exitNavigate configuration hierarchy

CLI Conventions & Modifiers

Accessing the CLI
admin@hostname:~$ cli
admin@hostname-cli>
Director-Managed Devices
On Director-managed devices, local commit is blocked by default. Local commits put the device out of sync with the Director.
# From bash shell:
vsh allow-cli
# Answer: yes
# Password: versa123
# Then enter CLI: cli → configure → commit works
Output Modifiers (Pipe Commands)
ModifierDescriptionExample
| match <pattern>Filter output lines containing patternshow configuration | match bgp
| nomoreDisable paginationshow route | nomore
| display setShow config as set commandsshow configuration | display set
| display xmlShow config in XML formatshow configuration | display xml
| countCount matching linesshow configuration | display set | count
Use | match (NOT | include). Use | nomore (NOT | no-more). These are ConfD-specific modifiers.
Configuration Display Paths
PurposePathExample
Configuration (set commands)orgs org-services <org>set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat ...
Operational (show commands)orgs org <org>show orgs org VERSA sessions ...
Interface Naming Convention
PrefixTypeExample
vni-X/YVersa Physical Interface (WAN/LAN)vni-0/0, vni-0/1
enet-X/YEthernet LAN Interface (22.1.1+)enet-0/5
ae<N>Aggregated Ethernet (LACP)ae0, ae1
tvi-X/YTunnel Virtual Interfacetvi-0/22
ptvi<N>Pseudo Tunnel Virtual Interface (SD-WAN overlay)ptvi421
eth-X/YManagement Interfaceeth-0/0
lo-X/YLoopback Interfacelo-0/0
irb<N>Integrated Routing and Bridging (L2/L3)irb1
gre-X/YGRE Tunnel Interfacegre-0/0
ipsec-X/YIPsec Tunnel Interfaceipsec-0/0
pppoe-X/YPPPoE Interfacepppoe-0/0
wwan-X/YWireless WAN (LTE/5G)wwan-0/0
wlan-X/YWiFi Interfacewlan-0/0
fab-X/YFabric Interfacefab-0/0
lt-X/YLoopback Tunnellt-0/0
Routing Instance Types
TypeDescriptionCLI Keyword
Virtual RouterStandard L3 routing instance (transport VRs)instance-type virtual-router
VRFL3VPN instance with RD/RT (enterprise tenant VRFs)instance-type vrf
Virtual SwitchL2 bridge domaininstance-type virtual-switch
MPLS VPN CoreControl plane VR for SD-WAN overlaympls-vpn-core
NAT Translation Types
TypeDescription
napt-44IPv4 Network Address Port Translation (most common for DIA)
nat-44Basic IPv4 NAT (1:1 static)
dynamic-nat-44Dynamic IPv4 NAT (source IP only, preserves port)
dnat-44Destination NAT IPv4 (port forwarding)
npt-66IPv6 Network Prefix Translation
nat-64IPv6 to IPv4 translation
twice-napt-44Twice NAPT (hairpin NAT)
Forwarding Classes (QoS)
ClassDescriptionDSCP
fc_ncNetwork Control (highest priority)CS6, CS7
fc_efExpedited Forwarding (real-time)EF, CS5
fc_afAssured Forwarding (business-critical)AF classes
fc_beBest Effort (default)Default, CS0

Configuration Hierarchy Tree

├── alarms # Alarm definitions and thresholds ├── interfaces # Physical, tunnel, aggregate, loopback interfaces │ ├── vni-X/Y # Versa physical interfaces │ ├── tvi-X/Y # Tunnel virtual interfaces │ ├── ae<N> # Aggregated Ethernet (LACP) │ ├── lo-X/Y # Loopback │ ├── eth-X/Y # Management │ ├── irb<N> # Integrated Routing & Bridging │ ├── gre-X/Y / ipsec-X/Y # Tunnel interfaces │ └── ptvi<N> # Pseudo Tunnel (SD-WAN overlay) ├── routing-instances # Virtual routers and VRFs │ └── <vr-name> │ ├── instance-type # virtual-router | vrf | virtual-switch │ ├── interfaces / networks # Assigned interfaces and networks │ ├── protocols # BGP, OSPF, OSPF3, RIP, PIM, IGMP │ ├── routing-options # Static routes, ECMP, policy routing │ └── policy-options # Redistribution, prefix-lists ├── networks # Network objects (parent-interface bindings) ├── orgs # Organizations (multi-tenancy) │ ├── org <org-name> # Tenant identity, services, SD-WAN site │ └── org-services <org-name> # Tenant service configurations │ ├── sd-wan # SLA profiles, forwarding profiles, policies │ ├── security # Access policies (NGFW rules) │ ├── cgnat # NAT pools and rules │ ├── ipsec # VPN profiles │ ├── class-of-service # QoS profiles, policies, app-QoS │ ├── lef # Log Export Function │ ├── objects # Zones, address objects, service objects │ ├── dhcp / dns-proxy # DHCP server/relay, DNS proxy │ ├── url-filtering / pbf # URL filtering, policy-based forwarding │ └── application-identification # Custom app signatures ├── system # System-wide configuration │ ├── identification # Hostname, location, GPS │ ├── sd-wan # SD-WAN site type, transport, controllers │ ├── vnf-manager # Director connectivity │ ├── session / services / users # Session, SSH/SFTP/WWW, local users │ └── dns / time-zone / login # DNS, timezone, fail2ban ├── protocols # BGP, OSPF, LLDP, BFD, VRRP, PIM, etc. ├── policy-options # Global routing policies ├── security / redundancy # Global security, HA config ├── ntp / snmp / crypto # NTP, SNMP, PKI certificates ├── resource-management # CPU/memory/flow distribution ├── dhcp-profiles # DHCP profile definitions ├── monitor / monitor-group # SLA monitoring objects ├── debug # 70+ subsystem debug levels └── guest-vnfs / virtual-wire / wlan # Guest VMs, virtual wire, WiFi

Config: BGP

91 COMMANDS
set protocols bgp 1
BGP configuration
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
aggregatePrefix Aggregation parameters
as-origination-intervalMinimum amount of time that must elapse between successive advertisements of UPDATE messages that report changes within the advertising BGP speaker's own autonomous system
as-overrideReplace Neighbor AS numbers in the AS-path with the Local AS Number
as-path-dottedProcess as-path in dotted notation(For example 1.1 for 65536) in all match policy configuration
bfd-liveness-detectionBidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) options
cluster-idCluster identifier
community-4-byteProcess the community in 4-byte format
dampingEnable route-flap dampening
debugConfigure debug flags
descriptionText description
disable-extended-message-length-capabilityDisable BGP extended message length capability
ebgp-preferencePreference(admin distance) for eBGP routes
enable-alarmsEnables alarm generation
familyProtocol family for NLRIs in updates
fast-external-falloverBring down the EBGP sessions for directly connected neighbors if the connected interface becomes down.
graceful-restartGraceful restart capability parameters
groupDefine a peer group
hold-timeHold time used when negotiating with a peer
ibgp-preferencePreference(admin distance) for iBGP routes
local-addressIP Address or the the name of interface the primary IP address of which should be used
local-asLocal autonomous system number
local-as-modeMode which decides the AS used for peering, AS numbers added in AS path while importing & exporting route when local-as is configured in group/neighbor
next-hop-unchangedKeep original nexthop of the BGP routes unchanged while sending to all EBGP neighbors.
outbound-route-filterDynamically negotiated cooperative route filtering
passiveDo not send open messages to a peer
passwordThe MD5 password used by this BGP instance <4..128 chars long>
path-selectionConfigure path selection strategy
peer-asRemote Autonomous system number in plain number or 'higher 16bits'.'Lower 16 bits' (asdot notation) format
peer-debugConfigure neighbor debug flags
prefix-limitPrefix-limit related parameters when importing routes from neighbor.
prefix-listCreate a prefix-list or enter prefix-list command mode
relax-first-as-checkRelax the check that the first(left-most) AS number in an AS path received from an EBGP neighbor is same as neighbor's AS
remove-privateRemove all private AS numbers from the received AS-path
route-flapConfigure route flap options for BGP
route-reflector-clientConfigure all neighbors in this instance as route reflector clients
router-idIPv4 address or name of the interface the primary IPv4 address of which should be used for BGP router-id
routing-peer-policyPeer/Peer-group policy configuration
set-stale-local-pref-in-ribConfigure local preference for stale routes installed in RIB
shutdownDisable all static neighbors in the BGP instance
sla-communityIt supports two formats.
sla-profileBGP SLA profile configuration
soft-reconfigurationKeep received routes even if rejected by routing-peer-policy.
sooSite of Origin Community to use while redistributing routes.
stale-routes-gr-behaviorIf enabled, stale routes shall be immune to route-deletes (default: disabled)
suppress-peer-asDo not advertise routes received from an EBGP neighbor to another neighbor in same AS as originating neighbor
ttlTTL value for the session (1..255)
set protocols bgp 1 aggregate
Configure prefix aggregation parameters. Allows summarization of specific routes into aggregate routes. Sub-parameters: family (inet/inet6), prefix, as-set, summary-only.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 aggregate <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
familyProtocol family for Aggregate routes
set protocols bgp 1 as-origination-interval
Minimum amount of time that must elapse between successive advertisements of UPDATE messages that report changes within the advertising BGP speaker's own autonomous system
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 as-origination-interval <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<1-65535>Seconds Default 15
Accepted Values
<1-65535>
set protocols bgp 1 as-override
Replace neighbor AS numbers in the AS-path with the local AS number. Used when sites have the same AS number (e.g., hub-and-spoke with same customer AS). Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 as-override <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 as-path-dotted
Process AS-path in dotted notation (e.g., 1.1 for 65536) in all match policy configuration. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 as-path-dotted <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<Default>false
Accepted Values
false,true
set protocols bgp 1 bfd-liveness-detection
Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) options. Configure BFD timers: minimum-interval, minimum-receive-interval, multiplier (default 3), transmit-interval-minimum-interval, transmit-interval-threshold.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 bfd-liveness-detection <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 cluster-id
Cluster identifier for route reflector. Typically the router-id of the route reflector. Required when multiple route reflectors exist in the same cluster.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 cluster-id <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 community-4-byte
Process the community attribute in 4-byte (extended) format instead of standard 2-byte format. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 community-4-byte <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 damping
Enable BGP route-flap dampening. When enabled, routes that flap repeatedly are suppressed. Configure half-life, reuse, suppress, and max-suppress-time values. Default: disabled.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 damping <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 debug
Configure debug flags for BGP troubleshooting. Options: all, bfd, error, event, filter, graceful-restart, keepalive, normal, notification, open, packet, policy, route, state, timer, update.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 debug <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 description
Text description for this BGP instance.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 description <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 disable-extended-message-length-capability
Disable BGP extended message length capability. When disabled, BGP messages are limited to 4096 bytes. Default: enabled (extended messages allowed).
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 disable-extended-message-length-capability <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 ebgp-preference
Administrative distance (preference) for eBGP routes. Lower values are preferred. Default: 20. Range: 1-4294967295.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 ebgp-preference <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 enable-alarms
Enable or disable alarm generation for BGP events such as neighbor state changes. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 enable-alarms <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family
Protocol address family for Network Layer Reachability Information (NLRIs) in BGP updates. Families: inet (unicast, multicast, versa-private, labeled-unicast), inet-vpn (unicast), inet6 (unicast), inet6-vpn (unicast), l2vpn (evpn).
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet unicast
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet unicast <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet unicast prefix-limit-control
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet unicast prefix-limit-control <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet multicast
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet multicast <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet versa-private
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet versa-private <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet labeled-unicast
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet labeled-unicast <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet-vpn
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet-vpn <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet-vpn unicast
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet-vpn unicast <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet6
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet6 <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet6 unicast
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet6 unicast <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet6-vpn
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet6-vpn <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family inet6-vpn unicast
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family inet6-vpn unicast <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family l2vpn
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family l2vpn <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 family l2vpn evpn
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 family l2vpn evpn <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 fast-external-fallover
Immediately tear down EBGP sessions when the directly connected interface goes down, instead of waiting for hold-time expiry. Default: true.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 fast-external-fallover <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 graceful-restart
Configure BGP graceful restart capability. Parameters: disable (disable GR), restart-time (max time to wait for peers, 1-3600s, default 120), stale-routes-time (max time to keep stale routes, 1-3600s, default 300).
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 graceful-restart <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
dynamic-peer-restart-timeMinimum time to wait for dynamic peers to reconnect after BGP process restarts
enableEnable graceful restart
familyProtocol family for enabling graceful restart parameters
helperGraceful restart helper enable/disable.
maximum-restart-timeSet the max time needed to restart and come back up
multiplierGraceful Restart time multiplier (1..255)
preserve-routes-across-restartsPreserve routes across restarts;; Default : disable
recovery-timeSet the estimated recovery time after a restart occurs
select-defer-timeThe maximum time (in seconds) following local restart that BGP process will wait before performing route selection
stalepath-timeMaximum time BGP waits before removing stale routes from a neighbor after neighbor session restarts
set protocols bgp 1 group
Define a BGP peer group. Groups contain common settings applied to all neighbors within the group. Parameters: type (internal/external), peer-as, local-address, neighbor, family, routing-peer-policy, bfd-liveness-detection, multihop, ttl, password, etc.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 group <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<name:string, min: 1 chars, max: 63 chars>
set protocols bgp 1 hold-time
Hold time in seconds used when negotiating with a peer. If no keepalive is received within hold-time, the session is declared down. Range: 0 or 3-65535. Default: 90. Value of 0 disables keepalives.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 hold-time <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 ibgp-preference
Administrative distance (preference) for iBGP routes. Lower values are preferred. Default: 200. Range: 1-4294967295.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 ibgp-preference <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 local-address
Source IP address or interface name for BGP sessions. The primary IP address of the specified interface is used as the source.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 local-address <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 local-as
Local autonomous system number for this BGP instance. Sub-parameter: as-number (1-4294967295 or asdot notation).
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 local-as <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 local-as as-number
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 local-as as-number <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 local-as-mode
Determines how the local-as is used when configured at group/neighbor level. Options: prepend (default, prepend local-as to AS path), no-prepend (do not prepend), replace-as (replace global AS with local-as).
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 local-as-mode <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 next-hop-unchanged
Keep original next-hop of BGP routes unchanged while sending to all EBGP neighbors. Useful in EBGP multihop or exchange point scenarios. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 next-hop-unchanged <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 outbound-route-filter
Enable dynamically negotiated cooperative route filtering (ORF). Allows a BGP speaker to send filter criteria to its peer, reducing unnecessary UPDATE messages.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 outbound-route-filter <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 passive
Do not send BGP OPEN messages to a peer. Wait for the peer to initiate the session. Useful for route servers. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 passive <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 password
MD5 authentication password for BGP sessions. Must be 4-128 characters. Both peers must use the same password.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 password <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 path-selection
Configure BGP best path selection strategy. Options: always-compare-med (compare MED across all paths regardless of AS), cisco-non-deterministic (non-deterministic path selection), as-path-ignore (ignore AS path length in best path selection), compare-router-id (use router-id as tiebreaker).
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 path-selection <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
always-compare-medAlways compare MED values, regardless of neighbor AS
as-path-ignoreExclude AS-path from BGP best path computation
as-path-multipath-relaxAllow differing AS paths to be considered for multipath if their lengths are equal.
cisco-non-deterministicUse Cisco IOS nondeterministic path selection algorithm
set protocols bgp 1 peer-as
Remote AS number of the BGP peer. Supports plain number (1-4294967295) or asdot notation (higher16bits.lower16bits).
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 peer-as <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 peer-debug
Configure per-neighbor debug flags. Same debug categories as instance-level debug but applied to specific peers.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 peer-debug <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 prefix-limit
Maximum number of prefixes accepted from a neighbor. Parameters: maximum (1-4294967295), teardown (tear down session when limit exceeded), teardown-percentage (warning threshold percentage, 1-100).
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 prefix-limit <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 prefix-list
Create or configure a prefix-list for route filtering. Used in routing-peer-policy import/export rules.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 prefix-list <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<WORD>Prefix list name
set protocols bgp 1 relax-first-as-check
Relax the check that the first (leftmost) AS number in AS path from an EBGP neighbor matches the neighbor's configured peer-as. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 relax-first-as-check <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 remove-private
Remove all private AS numbers (64512-65534, 4200000000-4294967294) from the AS path of routes received from EBGP peers. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 remove-private <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 route-flap
Configure route flap dampening parameters: half-life (1-45 min, default 15), reuse (1-20000, default 750), suppress (1-20000, default 2000), max-suppress-time (1-720 min, default 60).
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 route-flap <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
free-max-timeThe maximum time for which a penalty shall be remembered
reuse-array-sizeThis is the size of the reuse index arrays
reuse-max-timeThe time corresponding to the last reuse list
reuse-sizeThis is the number of reuse lists.
set protocols bgp 1 route-reflector-client
Configure all neighbors in this BGP instance as route reflector clients. The local router acts as a route reflector. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 route-reflector-client <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
aggregatePrefix Aggregation parameters
as-origination-intervalMinimum amount of time that must elapse between successive advertisements of UPDATE messages that report changes within the advertising BGP speaker's own autonomous system
as-overrideReplace Neighbor AS numbers in the AS-path with the Local AS Number
as-path-dottedProcess as-path in dotted notation(For example 1.1 for 65536) in all match policy configuration

dampingEnable route-flap dampening
debugConfigure debug flags

next-hop-unchangedKeep original nexthop of the BGP routes unchanged while sending to all EBGP neighbors.
outbound-route-filterDynamically negotiated cooperative route filtering

prefix-limitPrefix-limit related parameters when importing routes from neighbor.

relax-first-as-checkRelax the check that the first(left-most) AS number in an AS path received from an EBGP neighbor is same as neighbor's AS
remove-privateRemove all private AS numbers from the received AS-path
route-flapConfigure route flap options for BGP

sla-communityIt supports two formats.
sla-profileBGP SLA profile configuration
soft-reconfigurationKeep received routes even if rejected by routing-peer-policy.
sooSite of Origin Community to use while redistributing routes.
stale-routes-gr-behaviorIf enabled, stale routes shall be immune to route-deletes (default: disabled)

ttlTTL value for the session (1..255)
set protocols bgp 1 router-id
BGP router-id. Can be an IPv4 address or interface name (uses primary IPv4 address of the interface).
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 router-id <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 routing-peer-policy
Peer or peer-group routing policy configuration. Used to apply import/export policies for route filtering, attribute modification, and redistribution.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 routing-peer-policy <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
Policy
set protocols bgp 1 set-stale-local-pref-in-rib
Configure local preference value for stale routes installed in the RIB during graceful restart. Range: 0-4294967295.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 set-stale-local-pref-in-rib <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 shutdown
Administratively shut down all static neighbors in the BGP instance. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 shutdown <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 sla-community
It supports two formats. The first format supports extended community as 'sla:IPv4 address in dotted notation followed by 2 byte decimal with maximum value of 65535' or 'sla:4 byte decimal with a value between 1 and 4294967295 followed by letter L:2 byte decimal with a maximum value of 65535' or 'sla:2 byte decimal with a value between 1 and 65535:4 byte decimal with a maximum value o
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 sla-community <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<string>
Accepted Values
<string>
set protocols bgp 1 sla-profile
BGP SLA profile for path selection based on network performance metrics. Parameters: name, loss-threshold, latency-threshold, jitter-threshold.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 sla-profile <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
Configure
set protocols bgp 1 soft-reconfiguration
Keep received routes in memory even if rejected by routing-peer-policy. Enables soft reconfiguration inbound without clearing the BGP session. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 soft-reconfiguration <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 soo
Site of Origin community (format: IP:NN or ASN:NN) attached to routes when redistributed into BGP. Prevents routing loops in multi-homed sites.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 soo <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 stale-routes-gr-behavior
When enabled, stale routes during graceful restart are immune to route deletes. Default: disabled.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 stale-routes-gr-behavior <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 suppress-peer-as
Do not advertise routes received from an EBGP neighbor to another neighbor in the same AS as the originating neighbor. Prevents AS path loops. Default: false.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 suppress-peer-as <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 ttl
TTL value for BGP session packets. Range: 1-255. Default: 1 for EBGP (directly connected), 255 for IBGP. Set higher for EBGP multihop.
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 ttl <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 group test-grp
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 group test-grp <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 group test-grp type
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 group test-grp type <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 group test-grp peer-as
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 group test-grp peer-as <parameters>
set protocols bgp 1 group test-grp local-address
Syntax
set protocols bgp 1 group test-grp local-address <parameters>

Syntax

Protocol family for Aggregate routes

Parameters

inet6Configure IPv6 Aggregate routes

set protocols bgp <parameters>

editEdit a sub-element
exitExit from this level

loadLoad configuration from an ASCII file
moveMove a parameter
mustEvaluate xpath must expression
quitExit from this level

requestMake system-level requests
resolvedConflicts have been resolved

rollbackRoll back database to last committed version

Interface
Loopback
Management

ptvi422

set interfaces vni-0/0
Versa Physical interface
Syntax
set interfaces vni-0/0 <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
bandwidthInterface bandwidth in kbps
descriptionDescription for the interface
dhcp-trustedDHCP trusted port.
dynamic-interfaceDynamic port profile will be applied
enableEnable/disable this interface
ether-optionsSpecify Ethernet options
ethernet-segmentEVPN multihoming configuration for Ethernet segment
hold-timeHold the link state in down state for configured interval before declaring it as operational when transitioning from down to up state.
mirror-interfaceUsed for traffic mirroring
mtuMTU of the interface
native-vlan-idSpecifies the Native VLAN ID for the interface
outer-tpidOuter VLAN tag protocol id supported on the port
poePower over Ethernet
pppoe-base-interfaceUsed as PPPoE base interface
promiscuousEnable or disable promiscuous mode on this interface
sfp-optionsSpecify SFP options
standby-optionsStandby configuration
unitConfigure logical interfaces under this interface
virtual-wireMember of virtual wire pair

publish-address

parameters

smart-aggregator

System Identification
Syntax
set system identification <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
contactContact information of the person responsible for this node
descriptionTextual description of the entity
idUnique ID of this node
latitudeLatitude of the node's location
locationPhysical location of this node
longitudeLongitude of the node's location
nameAn administratively-assigned name for this node
objidObject ID (SNMPv2-MIB::system::sysObjectID)
servicesA value which indicates the set of services that this entity may potentially offer
set system vnf-manager
VNF Manager configuration for appliance
Syntax
set system vnf-manager <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
ip-addressesIPv4 addresses of VNF manager
vnf-mgmt-interfacesManagement interface: tvi, gre, gre6, eogre, ipsec, pppoe, vni, tdlo, t1e1 or ae
set system session
Session timeout configuration
Syntax
set system session <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
additional-flow-key-attributeAdditional attribute used to identify a flow
allow-unsupported-protocolOption to control IP Protocol validity check
check-tcp-synAllow the session creation only on existence of the TCP SYN flag
interim-updateInterim update parameters
limit-icmp-errorsLimit the rate of ICMP errors generated by VOS (per second) per worker thread
log-unknown-hostbound-packetsLog packets unknown packets destined to VOS IP address
reevaluate-reverse-flowRe-evaluate the packet policy for first packet of a reverse flow if the forward flow has been marked as drop
send-icmp-unreachableSend ICMP unreachable to source during route lookup failures
session-purge-batch-sizeNumber of sessions to inspect in each cycle of session aging process
session-reevaluateSession reevaluate during config/route change
tcp-adjust-mssEnable TCP MSS adjustment
tcp-secure-resetFollow the secure criteria for accepting RST packets
tcp-send-resetSend RST to the source if the first packet of a new TCP flow does not have SYN flag set
timeout-defaultDefault timeout for sessions in seconds
timeout-hardHard session timeout in seconds
timeout-icmpIdle timeout for ICMP sessions in seconds
timeout-tcpIdle timeout for TCP sessions in seconds
timeout-tcp-halfclosedTimeout for TCP sessions after receiving 1st FIN and before 2nd FIN or RST, in seconds
timeout-tcp-halfopenTimeout for TCP sessions after receiving SYN and before completion of 3-way handshake, in seconds
timeout-tcpwaitTCP wait timeout (after receiving 2 FIN or RST) in seconds
timeout-udpIdle timeout for UDP sessions in seconds

request system
System configuration and statistics
Syntax
request system <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
bw-measurementsystem level bandwidth measurement commands
configurationConfiguration actions for the appliance
cpu-watchdogCommand to enable the cpu hardware watchdog monitor
create-snapshotCreate a snapshot (known-good) state
db-backupTake periodic backup of config and operational data
dynamic-provisionSystem-level dynamic-provisioning commands
fips-mode
firmwareFirmware management for WiFi and T1/E1 cards on CSG devices
gpsShow device location
hypervisorHypervisor feature on baremetal (uCPE)
isolate-cpuIsolate CPUs from the kernel scheduler
isolcpuIsolate CPUs from the kernel scheduler (DEPRECATED)
kernel-crashdumpCommand to enable the kernel crash dump
load-defaultReset the appliance to default device configuration
loginConfigure login monitoring parameters
logoutUser operations
packagePackage management
platform-watchdogCommand to enable the platform hardware watchdog monitor
rebootReboot the appliance
resetReset the appliance to default device configuration
restartRestart Versa applications
rollbackRollback to a given snapshot (known-good state)
save-custom-defaultSave the current configuration as default device configuration
scaleinSystem scalein request
scaleoutSystem scaleout request
secure-mode
setSet system date and time
set-management-statusSet appliance status for management operations
shellShell lockout actions
shutdownShutdown the appliance
smartctlCommand to set the SSD smartmontools monitor
statusVersa application status
stopStop Versa applications
tech-supportGather system information to request technical support
trial-keyForce load a trial/evaluation key
unlock-userUnlock a locked user
user-lockoutConfigure user lockout(faillock)

Config: OSPF & OSPFv3

32 COMMANDS
set protocols ospf
Syntax
set protocols ospf <parameters>
set protocols ospf3
Syntax
set protocols ospf3 <parameters>
set protocols ospf 1
OSPF instance configuration
Syntax
set protocols ospf 1 <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
areaConfigure an OSPF area
default-informationConfiguration to announce default route
descriptionText description
enable-alarmsEnables alarm generation
ext-preferencePreference(admin distance) for external routes
int-preferencePreference(admin distance) for internal routes
reference-bandwidthBandwidth for calculating metric defaults
router-idThe router-id for this OSPF instance
set protocols ospf 1 area
Syntax
set protocols ospf 1 area <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
Area
set protocols ospf 1 default-information
Configuration to announce default route
Syntax
set protocols ospf 1 default-information <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
alwaysAnnounce default route to OSPF neighbors based on the default-information configuration of the presence of default route in the routing table
metricMetric value for the default route to be announced to OSPF neighbors
originateAnnounce default route to OSPF neighbors
prefix-listDefault route will be announced to OSPF neighbors based on the default-information configuration only if there are routes in the routing-table matching this prefix-list
tagTag value for the default route to be announced to OSPF neighbors
typeExternal type of the default route to be announced to OSPF neighbors
set protocols ospf 1 default-information always
Announce default route to OSPF neighbors based on the default-information configuration of the presence of default route in the routing table
Syntax
set protocols ospf 1 default-information always <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<Default>false
set protocols ospf 1 default-information metric
Syntax
set protocols ospf 1 default-information metric <parameters>
set protocols ospf 1 default-information originate
Announce default route to OSPF neighbors
Syntax
set protocols ospf 1 default-information originate <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<Default>false
set protocols ospf 1 default-information prefix-list
Syntax
set protocols ospf 1 default-information prefix-list <parameters>
set protocols ospf 1 default-information tag
Tag value for the default route to be announced to OSPF neighbors

set protocols ospf 1 default-information tag <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<Default>0
set protocols ospf 1 default-information type

set protocols ospf 1 default-information type <parameters>

Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<string, min: 0 chars, max: 127 chars>
Accepted Values
<string, min: 0 chars, max: 127 chars>

set protocols ospf 1 ext-preference

Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<1-255>Preference(admin distance);;Default 110

OSPFv3 instance configuration

Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
areaConfigure an OSPFv3 area
default-informationConfiguration to announce default route
descriptionText description
enable-alarmsEnables alarm generation
ext-preferencePreference(admin distance) for external routes
int-preferencePreference(admin distance) for internal routes
router-idThe router-id for this OSPF instance
vpn-pe-ce-instanceTo specify router as provider-edge(PE) while using OSPF between provider edge (PE) routers and customer edge (CE) routers in a BGP/MPLS IP VPN.

29 COMMANDS
set protocols bfd
Syntax
set protocols bfd <parameters>
set protocols lldp
Syntax
set protocols lldp <parameters>
set protocols vrrp
Syntax
set protocols vrrp <parameters>
set protocols bfd 1
Syntax
set protocols bfd 1 <parameters>
set protocols vrrp 1
Syntax
set protocols vrrp 1 <parameters>
set protocols rip 1
RIP instance configuration
Syntax
set protocols rip 1 <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
authentication-keyAuthentication plain text password (key of length 1 to 16)
authentication-typeAuthentication type
bfd-liveness-detectionBidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) options
groupDefine a group
holddownTime interval to delete a route from routing table after its expiry (1-255 seconds)
md5-authentication-keyMD5 Authentication password (key of length 4 to 16)
preferencePreference(admin distance) for RIP routes
receiveConfigure RIP receive options;;Default version-2
route-timeoutDelay before routes time out (1-255 seconds)
sendConfigure RIP send options ;;Default multicast
update-intervalInterval between regular route updates (1-2147483 seconds)
set protocols rip 1 authentication-key
Authentication plain text password (key of length 1 to 16)
Syntax
set protocols rip 1 authentication-key <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<PASSWORD>

Syntax
set protocols rip 1 authentication-type <parameters>

Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription

multiplierDetection time multiplier (1..255)
transmit-intervalTransmit-interval options

<1-255000>milliseconds

Config: PIM, IGMP & Multicast

52 COMMANDS
set protocols pim
Syntax
set protocols pim <parameters>
set protocols igmp
Syntax
set protocols igmp <parameters>
set protocols pim 1
PIM configuration
Syntax
set protocols pim 1 <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
assert-timeoutSet assert timeout in seconds(5..210);;Default 180
candidate-bsrConfigure a candidate BSR.
candidate-rpConfigure a candidate RP.
cluster-listList of cluster-ids to which the pim instance belongs.
enable-alarmsEnables alarm generation
interfacePIM interface options
join-prune-timeoutSet join/prune timeout in seconds(210..420);;Default 210
override-intervalOverride interval value in milliseconds(500..6000 milliseconds);;Default 2500
pim-mrpfRIB for PIM RPF lookup;;Default unicast
propagation-delayPropagation delay value in milliseconds(250..2000 milliseconds);;Default 500
register-message-filterRegister filter options
rpRendezvous point properties
spt-thresholdSet shortest-path-tree threshold
ssm-groupsGroup address range of SSM
set protocols pim 1 assert-timeout
Set assert timeout in seconds(5..210);;Default 180

set protocols igmp 1
IGMP configuration
Syntax
set protocols igmp 1 <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
interfaceInterface options for IGMP
query-intervalConfigure genral purpose query interval (2..31744 seconds);;Default 125
query-last-member-intervalConfigure group specific query interval (1..25 seconds);;Default 1
query-response-intervalThe query response interval (1..25 seconds);;Default 10
robust-countExpected packet loss on a subnet(2..7);;Default 2
set protocols igmp 1 interface
Interface name
Syntax
set protocols igmp 1 interface <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<Ethernet unit Name (enet-X/X.[0-4095])>
<ae subinterface name (aeX.[0-4095])>

<lt subinterface name (lt-[0-15]/[0-199999].[0-4095])>
<tvi subinterface name (tvi-X/X.[0-4095])>

<dsl subinterface name (dsl-X/X.[0-4095])>
<geneve subinterface name (geneve-X/X.[0-4095])>
<lt subinterface name (lt-X/X.[0-4095])>
<vni or t1e1 subinterface name (vni-X/X.[0-4095]) or t1e1-X/X.[0-4095]>
Interface
set protocols igmp 1 query-interval
Configure genral purpose query interval (2..31744 seconds);;Default 125
Syntax
set protocols igmp 1 query-interval <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<unsignedInt, 2 .. 31744>
Accepted Values
<unsignedInt, 2 .. 31744>
set protocols igmp 1 query-last-member-interval
Syntax
set protocols igmp 1 query-last-member-interval <parameters>
set protocols igmp 1 query-response-interval
The query response interval (1..25 seconds);;Default 10
Syntax
set protocols igmp 1 query-response-interval <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
<unsignedInt, 1 .. 25>
Accepted Values
<unsignedInt, 1 .. 25>
set protocols igmp 1 robust-count
Syntax
set protocols igmp 1 robust-count <parameters>

Config: Routing Options & Policy Options

138 COMMANDS
set routing-options
Protocol-independent routing option config
Syntax
set routing-options <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
create-dynamic-gre-tunnelsCreate dynamic GRE tunnels for L3 VPN endpoint PEs
evpn-alarmsEnables EVPN alarms
evpn-local-router-addressLocal router address used as BGP nexthop for EVPN routes
evpn-local-router-interfaceLocal router interface address used as BGP nexthop for EVPN routes
intelligent-traffic-directorConfigure intelligent-traffic-director functionality
mpls-vpn-local-router-addressLocal router address used as BGP nexthop for VPN routes
mpls-vpn-local-router-interfaceLocal router interface address used as BGP nexthop for VPN routes
ribrib specific configuration
staticStatic routes
set policy-options
Routing policy option configuration
Syntax
set policy-options <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
instance-importDefine redistribution between routing-instances for an AFI and Unicast SAFI
instance-import-afi-safiDefine redistribution between routing-instances for an AFI and SAFI
prefix-listCreate a prefix-list or enter prefix-list command mode
redistribute-toEnable route redistibution
redistribute-to-bgpName of policy used for redistribution to BGP
redistribute-to-bgp6Name of policy used for redistribution to BGP4+
redistribute-to-ospfName of policy used for redistribution to OSPF
redistribute-to-ospf3Name of policy used for redistribution to OSPFv3
redistribute-to-ripName of policy used for redistribution to RIP
redistribution-policyRedistribution policy configuration
set routing-options create-dynamic-gre-tunnels
Protocol-independent routing option config
Syntax
set routing-options create-dynamic-gre-tunnels <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
evpn-alarmsEnables EVPN alarms
evpn-local-router-addressLocal router address used as BGP nexthop for EVPN routes
evpn-local-router-interfaceLocal router interface address used as BGP nexthop for EVPN routes
intelligent-traffic-directorConfigure intelligent-traffic-director functionality
mpls-vpn-local-router-addressLocal router address used as BGP nexthop for VPN routes
mpls-vpn-local-router-interfaceLocal router interface address used as BGP nexthop for VPN routes
ribrib specific configuration
staticStatic routes

disabled

set orgs org-services VERSA crypto pki settings certificate-expiry
Certificate expiry alarm related settings
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA crypto pki settings certificate-expiry <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
alert-beforeRaises alarm before this number of days
alert-dailyRaises alarm every day only for last week before expiry and until the certificate is replaced
repeat-intervalRepeats the alarm at this interval (in days)
set orgs org-services VERSA crypto pki settings certificate-expiry alert-before

set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl
Top level container for TWAMP configuration
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
clientConfiguration of the TWAMP Control-Client logical entity.
serverConfiguration of the TWAMP Server logical entity.
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl client
Configuration of the TWAMP Control-Client logical entity.
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl client <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
client-enableIndicates whether the device is allowed to operate as a TWAMP Control-Client.
client-timeoutThe timeout value for the TWAMP-Client to wait for reply from TWAMP-Server before closing connection.
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl client client-enable
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl client client-enable <parameters>
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl client client-timeout
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl client client-timeout <parameters>
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl server
Configuration of the TWAMP Server logical entity.
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl server <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
server-enableIndicates whether the device is allowed to operate as a TWAMP Server.
server-waitTWAMP control session timeout in seconds after which server closes connection
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-cntrl server server-enable
Syntax

set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light
Top level container for TWAMP-Light configuration
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
lefTWAMP-Light LEF container
twamp-light-session-reflectorTWAMP-Light Session-Reflector container
twamp-light-session-senderTWAMP-Light Session-Sender container
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light twamp-light-session-reflector
TWAMP-Light Session-Reflector container
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light twamp-light-session-reflector <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
ref-waitREFWAIT(TWAMP test session timeout in seconds), the default value is 900
reflector-light-enableWhether this network element is enabled to act as TWAMP-Light Reflector
reflector-light-mode-stateThe state of the mode of the TWAMP-Light Session-Reflector
test-sessionThis structure is a container of test session managed objects
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light twamp-light-session-reflector ref-wait
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light twamp-light-session-reflector ref-wait <parameters>
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light twamp-light-session-reflector reflector-light-enable
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light twamp-light-session-reflector reflector-light-enable <parameters>
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light twamp-light-session-reflector reflector-light-mode-state
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light twamp-light-session-reflector reflector-light-mode-state <parameters>
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light twamp-light-session-reflector test-session
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA twamp-light twamp-light-session-reflector test-session <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
twamp

Syntax

Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
sender-light-enableWhether this network element is enabled to act as TWAMP-Light Sender
test-sessionThis structure is a container of test session managed objects

Syntax

Services: CGNAT

18 COMMANDS
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat
Carrier Grade NAT configuration
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
poolsNAT pools config
rulesNAT rules config
softwireSoftwire configuration
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools
NAT pools config
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools <parameters>
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat rules
NAT rules config
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat rules <parameters>
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat softwire
Softwire configuration
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat softwire <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
mape
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat softwire mape
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat softwire mape <parameters>
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools DIA-Pool-INET-1
List of NAT pools
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools DIA-Pool-INET-1 <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
addressAddress and prefix (DEPRECATED, use address-list)
address-allocationAddress allocation scheme
address-listList of addresses and/or prefixes
address-rangeSource address range (DEPRECATED, use address-range-list)
address-range-listSource address range
descriptionPool description
destination-port-rangeDestination port range
egress-interfaceInterface to be used for source interface NAT
egress-networkNetwork to be used for source interface NAT
icmp-mapping-timeoutICMP mapping timeout in seconds
provider-orgPublic addresses are borrowed from this pool
referenced-outside-natIs this pool referenced outside NAT?
routing-instanceRouting instance for this pool
source-portSource port translation config
tcp-mapping-timeoutTCP mapping timeout in seconds
udp-mapping-timeoutUDP mapping timeout in seconds
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools DIA-Pool-INET-1 source-port
Source port translation config
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools DIA-Pool-INET-1 source-port <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
allocation-schemePort allocation scheme
block-reserved-portsBlock reserved ports
exclude-port-rangePort range to be excluded from the NAT pool
random-allocationAllocate IP/port randomly
rangePort range to be used for translation
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools DIA-Pool-INET-1 source-port allocation-scheme
Port allocation scheme
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat pools DIA-Pool-INET-1 source-port allocation-scheme <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
automaticAutomatic ports assignment
range-basedAllocate port from range
Accepted Values
automatic,range-based
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat rules DIA-Rule-VERSA-Enterprise-INET-1
Syntax

Accepted Values
automatic,range-based
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat rules DIA-Rule-VERSA-Enterprise-INET-1 then translated
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat rules DIA-Rule-VERSA-Enterprise-INET-1 then translated <parameters>
Accepted Values
automatic,range-based

Syntax

Accepted Values
automatic,range-based

set orgs org-services VERSA cgnat rules DIA-Rule-INET-1 from

Services: Class of Service (QoS)

73 COMMANDS
set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service

Syntax

Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
app-qos-policiesApplication QOS policy rules configuration
drop-profilesclass-of-service configuration for drop profiles
forwarding-class-mapMapping from forwarding-class to traffic-class and queue number
ingress-shapingIngress shaping configuration
interfacesclass-of-service configuration for interfaces
networksclass-of-service configuration for networks
qos-policiesQOS policy rules configuration
qos-profilesQOS profile configuration
rw-rulesrewrite rules config
scheduler-mapsclass-of-service configuration for scheduler-maps
schedulersclass-of-service configuration for scheduler

Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
Name

Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service drop-profiles <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
Drop
set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service forwarding-class-map
Mapping from forwarding-class to traffic-class and
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service forwarding-class-map <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
fc1Forwarding Class 1
fc2Forwarding Class 2
fc3Forwarding Class 3
fc5Forwarding Class 5
fc6Forwarding Class 6
fc7Forwarding Class 7
fc9Forwarding Class 9
fc10Forwarding Class 10
fc11Forwarding Class 11
fc13Forwarding Class 13
fc14Forwarding Class 14
fc15Forwarding Class 15
fc_afForwarding Class 8 (assured-forwarding)
fc_beForwarding Class 12 (best-effort)
fc_efForwarding Class 4 (expedited-forwarding)
fc_ncForwarding Class 0 (network-control)
set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service forwarding-class-map fc1
Forwarding classes
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA class-of-service forwarding-class-map fc1 <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
queueQueue Number within the traffic class
traffic-classTraffic class

Services: LEF (Logging)

20 COMMANDS
set orgs org-services VERSA dhcp dhcp4-server-and-relay lef-profile

set orgs org-services VERSA security captive-portal
Captive portal
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA security captive-portal <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
anchoringCapitve portal anchoring type
ask-formDirectory location for the form to be displayed for ask action response
auth-failed-pageDirectory location of the page to be displayed after authentication request fails
auth-formDirectory location for the form to be displayed for authentication action response
block-pageDirectory location of the page to be displayed after blocking the request
cancel-pageDirectory location of the page to be displayed after user has canceled the action
cookie-auth-profileAuthentication profile for cookie authentication
custom-redirect-parametersList of query parameters used while applying custom redirect
expiration-timeExpiration time for the pages (in minutes), pages will be displayed to the user once during this period
inform-formDirectory location for the form to be displayed for inform action response
justify-formDirectory location for the form to be displayed for justify action response
override-formDirectory location for the form to be displayed for override action response
provider-orgProvider org to look for captive portal pages if pages are not configured here
service-endpointsCaptive portal service endpoint configuration
ssl-ca-certificateCertificate to use for captive portal over SSL
track-by-hostCaptive portal tracking by host

set orgs org-services VERSA security captive-portal custom-redirect-parameters
List of query parameters used while applying custom redirect
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA security captive-portal custom-redirect-parameters <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
actionName of parameters which will specify applied action object name value
mac-addressName of parameters which will specify MAC address name value
security-policy-ruleName of parameters which will specify security access policy rule name value
source-ipName of parameters which will specify source IP address value
timeName of parameters which will specify time value
urlName of parameters which will specify URL value
url-categoryName of parameters which will specify URL-category value
url-reputationName of parameters which will specify URL-reputation value
urlf-profileName of parameters which will specify url-filtering profile name value
wan-ipName of parameters which will specify Versa WAN interface IP address name value

set orgs org-services VERSA layer2-sd-wan
SD-WAN policies configuration for Layer 2 traffic
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA layer2-sd-wan <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
application-cacheEnable/disable application detection using cache at the beginning of flow
application-dynamic-detectionEnable/disable application detection dynamically in the middle of flow
forwarding-profilesForwarding profiles to select outgoing link based on SLA requirements
policiesSD-WAN policies configuration for Layer 2 traffic
sla-profilesService Level Agreement (SLA) profiles configuration
url-category-cacheEnable/disable URL category detection using cache at the beginning of flow

set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering

Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
settingsURL filtering settings
url-trackingTrack URLs based on categories
user-defined-url-categoriesDefine user defined URL categories
set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering settings
URL filtering settings
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering settings <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
cloud-lookupURL-filtering cloud lookup configuration
historyStore URLF session history matching the filters; Matches all, If no filters are configured
loggingURL-filtering logging related settings
match-typeField used to match URL category
spackSpack configurations
walled-gardenThis configuration governs URL categories that need access restrictions prior to authentication
set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering settings cloud-lookup
URL-filtering cloud lookup configuration
Syntax
set orgs org-services VERSA url-filtering settings cloud-lookup <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
cache-limitMaximum number of cloud response cache values to store locally
cache-time-to-liveCloud response cache TTL value, in seconds
cloud-lookup-profileCloud lookup profile to use while connecting to cloud server
modeMode to use for cloud lookup request
query-parameterEnable/Disable query-parameter
stateEnable/Disable URL cloud lookup for all traffic
timeoutCloud request timeout, in milliseconds

Syntax
show orgs org | <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
appendAppend output text to a file
compareShow differences to running config
context-matchContext match
countCount the number of lines in the output
csvEmit table output in CSV format
de-selectSelect columns to not include
detailsDisplay details
displayDisplay options
display-levelDisplay level
exceptShow only text that does not matches a pattern
extendedShow referring elements
findSearch for the first occurrence of a pattern
grepCase insensitive search
hideHide display options
linnumEnumerate lines in the output
matchShow only text that matches a pattern
match-allAll selected filters must match
match-anyAt least one filter must match
morePaginate output
nomoreSuppress pagination
saveSave output text to a file
selectSelect additional columns
sort-bySelect sorting indices
tabEnforce table output
untilDisplay until the first occurrence of a pattern

show orgs org VERSA sessions

Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
briefDisplay sessions info in brief
detailDisplay sessions info in detail
extensiveDisplay extensive sessions info
filterDisplay sessions matching filter
ipsDisplay IPS sessions info
layer2Display Layer 2 sessions info
natDisplay NAT sessions info
npuDisplay NPU sessions information

sdwan-flowDisplay a single session given SDWAN flow key
session-idDisplay a single session matching ID
sfcDisplay SFC sessions info
summaryDisplay sessions summary
tcp-terminatedDisplay information about TCP-terminated sessions
vfpDisplay VFP sessions info

show bgp
Display BGP information
Syntax
show bgp <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
flap-statsDisplay the details of damped routes
graceful-restartDisplay BGP graceful-restart information
groupDisplay the BGP group database
mpls-vpn-core-infoDisplays mpls core bgp information
neighborDisplay BGP neighbor information
summaryDisplay a summary of BGP neighbor status
show bgp neighbor
Displays Tenant bgp information
Syntax
show bgp neighbor <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
briefBrief neighbor information
detailDetailed neighbor information
org

show bgp routing-instance <parameters>
show ospf
Display OSPF information
Syntax
show ospf <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
databaseShow OSPF link-state database
interfaceDisplay OSPF interface information
neighborDisplay OSPF neighbor information
overviewDisplay OSPF overview information
sham-linksDisplay OSPF sham link information
virtual-linksDisplay OSPF virtual links information
show ospf neighbor
Displays Tenant ospf neighbors information
Syntax
show ospf neighbor <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
briefDisplay brief OSPF neighbor information
detailDisplay detailed OSPF neighbor information
extensiveDisplay extensive OSPF neighbor information
org

overviewDisplay OSPFv3 overview information
virtual-linksDisplay OSPFv3 virtual links information

routing-instanceDisplay all ARP entries per routing instance

Show: System & Platform

160 COMMANDS
show system
System configuration and statistics
Syntax
show system <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
backtraceDisplay backtrace from coredump
bridge-addressSystem bridge address
bw-measurementsystem level bandwidth measurement commands
capabilitiesDisplay FlexVNF capability/feature
coredumpsList Of Coredumps
detailsDisplay system details
gpsShow device location
load-statsLoad Information
locked-usersList all locked users
managment-statusDisplay management status of appliance
package-infoVersa Package Information
partitionsDisplay Disk space statistics
platformPlatform specific configurations
poePower over ethernet
resourcesDisplay system resources
security
snapshotsDisplay snapshots (if created)
statisticsSystem statistics
statusVersa application status
storageDisplay block device statistics
trial-infoDisplay trial/evaluation information
uptimeUptime of versa-service
show system details
Syntax
show system details <parameters>
show system package-info
Syntax
show system package-info <parameters>
show system uptime
Display per tenant information
Syntax
show system uptime <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
detailIP information of active interfaces
briefDisplay interfaces per org
dynamic-tunnelsDisplay dynamic tunnels for organization
statisticsDisplay interfaces per org
show device
Connected device information
Syntax
show device <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
alarmOrg alarm statistics
clientsConnected device information
cpuloadDevice CPU load information
hardwareDevice hardware info
hardware-v2Device hardware info v2
led-sequenceLED blink sequence and their meaning (on branch device)
reset-mapReset key bindings
sensorsShow system sensors (fan-speed/cpu-temp etc)
softwareDevice software information
trial-infoDisplay trial/evaluation information
show device cpuload
Device CPU load information
Syntax
show device cpuload <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
CPU0
CPU
ID
CPU1
CPU2
CPU3
show device clients
Syntax
show device clients <parameters>
show alarms
Alarm configuration
Syntax
show alarms <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
allShow all alarms from /var/log/versa/alarms and /var/log/versa/alarm_local
destinationsDisplay alarm destination
last-nPick newest N alarms
localShow only local alarms from /var/log/versa/alarm_local
statisticsDisplay alarm statistics

start-timeStart processing logs from this timestamp (ISO date format)

show log tallylog
Syntax
show log tallylog <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
end-timeStop processing logs after this timestamp (ISO date format)
rawShow raw file (log which does not follow ISO date format)
reverseReverse chronological order
start-timeStart processing logs from this timestamp (ISO date format)
Skipping
show log wtmp
Syntax
show log wtmp <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
end-timeStop processing logs after this timestamp (ISO date format)
rawShow raw file (log which does not follow ISO date format)
reverseReverse chronological order
start-timeStart processing logs from this timestamp (ISO date format)
Skipping

aggregated-ethernet-optionsSpecify options for this Aggregated Ethernet link
bandwidthInterface bandwidth in kbps

dynamic-interfaceDynamic port profile will be applied
enableEnable/disable this interface
ether-optionsSpecify Ethernet options

mirror-interfaceUsed for traffic mirroring
modeInterface mode
mtuMTU of the interface
native-vlan-idSpecifies the Native VLAN ID for the interface

promiscuousEnable or disable promiscuous mode on this interface

sfp-optionsSpecify SFP options
standby-optionsStandby configuration

typeTunnel type

show configuration routing-instances
Routing instance configuration
Syntax
show configuration routing-instances <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
GUEST
Possible
instance-typeType of routing instance;;Default virtual-router
scalable-group-tagScalable group tag configuration
bridge-optionsBridge options
policy-optionsRouting policy option configuration
bridge-domainsbridge domain global configuration
usage-typeUsage type of routing instance
global-vrf-idGlobal vrf id of routing instance.
traffic-director-global-vrf-idSecond Global vrf id of routing instance.
networksList of network names
mpls-vpn-coreThis routing instance can be used as mpls-vpn-core-instance for other VRFs
mpls-vpn-core-instanceName of MPLS transport routing instance for this VRF; specify 'global' to use the default instance rather than a specific named routing-instance as the core instance
vrf-evpn-core-instanceName of the EVPN core routing instance responsible for BGP signaling of this VRF; specify 'global' to use the default instance rather than a specific named routing-instance as the core instance
reverse-traffic-instanceName of routing instance reverse flow creation in the case of intelligent-traffic-director; specify 'global' to use the default instance
evpn-coreThis routing instance can be used as core-instance for virtual-switch routing-instances with EVPN
cloud-export-instanceThis routing instance can be used for Cloud File export
descriptionText description of routing instance
interfacesList of Interface names
route-distinguisherRoute distinguisher for this instance.
vrf-import-targetTarget community to use when filtering L3VPN routes on import.
vrf-export-targetTarget community to use when exporting L3VPN routes.
vrf-both-targetTarget community to use when exporting L3VPN routes and when filtering them on import.
vnivxlan VNI to be signaled through EVPN for this IP VRF
routing-optionsProtocol-independent routing option configuration
protocolsRouting protocol configuration

show commit list
Syntax
show commit list <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
Number
alarmsAlarm configuration
aliasCreate command alias.
application-monitorsApplication monitor configuration
confdConfigConfD configuration.
cryptoDefault crypto commands
debugDebug logging configuration
dhcp-profilesDHCP profile info
elastic-policiesElastic policies
eventEvent scripts
guest-vnfsGuest Virtual Network Functions
interfacesInterfaces configuration
key-chainsAll configured key-chains on the device
monitorMonitor configuration
monitor-groupMonitor group configuration
nacmAccess control
networksNetworks configuration
ntpNTP configuration
orgsOrganizations
policy-optionsRouting policy option configuration
predefined-languagesPredefined Languages
protocolsRouting protocol configuration
redundancyRedundancy Configuration
resource-managementResource-management and flow distribution configuration
routing-instancesRouting instance config
routing-optionsProtocol-independent routing option config
securitySecurity configuration
service-node-group-clustersService node group cluster
service-node-groupsService Node Gateway Services Configuration
snmpSimple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) configuration
systemSystem configuration and statistics
t1e1T1/E1 PPP and MLPPP authentication protocol configuration
twamp-monitorTWAMP monitor configuration
userUser specific command aliases and default CLI session parameters
virtual-wireVirtual Wire configuration
webuiWeb UI specific configuration
wlanWiFi configuration and client details

Show: Security & NAT

43 COMMANDS
show security
Security configuration
Syntax
show security <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
avAntivirus definition information
geoipGeoIP Information
ip-filteringIP filtering related information
ipsIPS related information
ips-rulesShow IPS signatures
osspackOS Security package
scannersIPS scanners related info
security-packageSecurity package configuration
url-filteringURL Filtering Information
show crypto
Default crypto commands
Syntax
show crypto <parameters>
Parameters
Parameter / KeywordDescription
pkiDefault crypto PKI commands
crypto
ca-chain-data
use-with-default-ca-chain

Quick Reference: Common Operations

Day-1 Verification Commands
TaskCommand
Check BGP neighborsshow bgp neighbor brief
Check BGP summaryshow bgp summary
Show routing tableshow route
Show route in specific VRshow route routing-instance <VR>
Check OSPF neighborsshow ospf neighbor brief
Check interfacesshow interfaces
Check ARP tableshow arp
Check BFD sessionsshow bfd
View active sessionsshow orgs org <ORG> sessions brief
Check NAT sessionsshow orgs org <ORG> sessions nat
View SD-WAN tunnelsshow orgs org <ORG> sd-wan
Check system healthshow system
View alarmsshow alarms
Show running configshow configuration | display set | nomore
Troubleshooting
TaskCommand
Ping from VRping <IP> routing-instance <VR>
Traceroute from VRtraceroute <IP> routing-instance <VR>
Packet capturetcpdump interface <intf>
Clear BGP neighborrequest clear bgp neighbor <IP>
Clear ARPrequest clear arp
Clear sessionsrequest clear flow all org <ORG>
Reboot devicerequest system reboot
Check processesshow processes
View logsshow log
Unblock SSH IPrequest clear jail ssh ip <IP>

Quick Reference: Common Configuration

BGP Neighbor Configuration
# Configure an eBGP neighbor
set protocols bgp 1 group EBGP-PEERS type external
set protocols bgp 1 group EBGP-PEERS peer-as 65001
set protocols bgp 1 group EBGP-PEERS local-address 10.0.0.1
set protocols bgp 1 group EBGP-PEERS neighbor 10.0.0.2
set protocols bgp 1 group EBGP-PEERS family inet unicast
set protocols bgp 1 group EBGP-PEERS bfd-liveness-detection true
set protocols bgp 1 local-as 65000
Interface Configuration
# Configure a VNI interface with IPv4 and IPv6
set interfaces vni-0/0 unit 0 family inet address 192.168.1.1/24
set interfaces vni-0/0 unit 0 family inet6 address 2001:db8::1/64
set interfaces vni-0/0 mtu 9000
set interfaces vni-0/0 enable true
set interfaces vni-0/0 description "WAN Uplink"
NAPT-44 (Source NAT for DIA)
# Create NAT pool and rule for internet access
set orgs org-services <ORG> cgnat pools DIA-Pool routing-instance <VR>
set orgs org-services <ORG> cgnat pools DIA-Pool egress-network <NET>
set orgs org-services <ORG> cgnat pools DIA-Pool source-port allocation-scheme automatic

set orgs org-services <ORG> cgnat rules DIA-Rule from zone TRUST
set orgs org-services <ORG> cgnat rules DIA-Rule then translated translation-type napt-44
set orgs org-services <ORG> cgnat rules DIA-Rule then translated pool DIA-Pool
Security Access Policy (NGFW)
# Create an access policy rule
set orgs org-services <ORG> security access-policies Default-Policy rules Permit_All
set orgs org-services <ORG> security access-policies Default-Policy rules Permit_All match source zone TRUST
set orgs org-services <ORG> security access-policies Default-Policy rules Permit_All match destination zone UNTRUST
set orgs org-services <ORG> security access-policies Default-Policy rules Permit_All set action allow
set orgs org-services <ORG> security access-policies Default-Policy rules Permit_All set lef event both
SD-WAN SLA Profile
# Create an SLA profile for business-critical traffic
set orgs org-services <ORG> sd-wan sla-profiles Business-Critical loss 1
set orgs org-services <ORG> sd-wan sla-profiles Business-Critical latency 100
set orgs org-services <ORG> sd-wan sla-profiles Business-Critical jitter 30
System SD-WAN Site
# Configure SD-WAN site parameters
set system sd-wan site site-id 100
set system sd-wan site site-type spoke
set system sd-wan site provider-org <ORG>
set system sd-wan site chassis-id <UUID>
set system sd-wan site wan-interfaces <interface>

Quick Reference: Placeholders & Notes

Placeholder Conventions
PlaceholderMeaning
<ORG>Organization/tenant name (e.g., VERSA)
<VR>Routing instance name (e.g., INET-1-Transport-VR)
<interface>Interface name (e.g., vni-0/0.0)
<IP>IPv4 or IPv6 address
<ASN>BGP Autonomous System Number
<NET>Network object name
1 (in BGP path)BGP instance ID (always 1 on FlexVNF)
